title: Authentic Jamaican Jerk Tofu
rawTitle: ### Authentic Jamaican Jerk Tofu — Make It Dairy Free
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://makeitdairyfree.com/authentic-jamaican-jerk-tofu-recipe/ ✓ VERIFIED *(Andrew Bernard's family recipe with mom's input — properly authentic Jamaican jerk paste from scratch: pimenta/allspice berries, scotch bonnets, ground thyme, scallions, ginger, lime, soy sauce, molasses or browning, garam masala. **Pro tip from the recipe: freeze the tofu first for meat-like texture.** Marinate 4–6 hr min, ideally overnight; grill at 400°F or pan-fry 8–10 min. Recipe also notes leftover marinade keeps 2–3 months refrigerated and can be reduced with cornstarch into a glaze — useful for the at-pass finish on the Chunk version below.)*
- $2.30/serving · **Menu price:** $13–16 · 280 g · 380 cal · 20 g · 60 min + marinate · 4 · **Easy** (marinade bulk; sear/grill per ticket) · 🥘 ⚡
- **Premium row:** Chunk Pulled jerked +$2.10
urlNote: Andrew Bernard's family recipe with mom's input — properly authentic Jamaican jerk paste from scratch: pimenta/allspice berries, scotch bonnets, ground thyme, scallions, ginger, lime, soy sauce, molasses or browning, garam masala. **Pro tip from the recipe: freeze the tofu first for meat-like texture.** Marinate 4–6 hr min, ideally overnight; grill at 400°F or pan-fry 8–10 min. Recipe also notes leftover marinade keeps 2–3 months refrigerated and can be reduced with cornstarch into a glaze — useful for the at-pass finish on the Chunk version below.
description: Easy — marinade bulk; sear/grill per ticket · bulk-prep, fast-service.
ingredients: 14 oz block extra firm tofu, (frozen, then thawed and pressed)
1/4-1/2 cup packed brown sugar ((sub coconut sugar for low carb))
1 1/2 tbsp ground thyme ((or 2 tbsp fresh thyme leaves))
1 tbsp garam masala ((sub with five spice powder))
1-2 tbsp olive oil, (see notes if grilling )
steps: Freeze and thaw tofu and use a tofu press to get the water out. *See Notes in post for why and how to do this if needed.
Add all the ingredients except the tofu into a blender or food processor and blend until very well combined. Put into a jar.
Once pressed, slice tofu in half and then half again for 4 equal size pieces (or cube if desired).

Place tofu into a shallow container that has a lid. Spread 1/4-1/3 cup jerk sauce across and over all sides. Rub into the tofu. Place lid on top and into fridge to marinate overnight or at least 4-6 hours.

To Grill: Preheat grill to medium (about 400 degrees). Add tofu directly to greased grill grates or to a grill mat. Cook for 8-10 minutes, flipping halfway. To Pan Fry: add 1 tbsp oil to a large skillet and heat over medium low heat. Once hot, add tofu and cook for 8-10 minutes, flipping occasionally to get all the sides.
Remove and serve with Jamaican rice and peas or desired side dishes.

title: Jamaican Rice and Peas (Cruelty-free)
rawTitle: ### Vegan Jamaican Rice and Peas — Make It Dairy Free
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://makeitdairyfree.com/jamaican-rice-and-peas/ ✓ VERIFIED
- $1.10/serving · **Menu price:** $4–6 (side) · 220 g · 340 cal · 9 g · 60 min · 6 · **Easy** · 🥘 ⚡
description: Long-grain rice simmered in coconut milk with kidney beans, scotch bonnet, thyme, and allspice. Naturally vegan; the foundation of any Jamaican plate.
ingredients: 1/2 tsp vegan cane sugar, (more or less to taste (optional))
3/4 cup dried kidney beans, (soaked overnight, then drained (or 1 15oz can drained and rinsed) (133g))
1 1/2 cups white rice, (rinsed well (or brown basmati rice) (300g))
1 1/4 cups water ((can sub with low-sodium vegetable stock) (284g))
steps: In a large sauce pan over medium heat, add oil and heat until ready. Then add onions, garlic, scallions, thyme, scotch bonnet pepper, sugar (if using), black pepper, and allspice berries. Saute.

Once onions are soft (about 2-3 minutes), turn heat up to medium-high. Add in the kidney beans and coconut milk and stir together. Once mixture starts to boil, add the salt, stir, and reduce heat to low/simmer. Cover the pot and cook until beans are tender, about 30 minutes to an hour, stirring occasionally. - Note: If there is not enough liquid while simmering add an additional 1/2 of water to keep beans covered

Add in the rice and the 1 1/4 cup water and stir well. Bring to a boil, cover, then continue to simmer until rice is fluffy and liquid is soaked up(about 25-30 minutes). Remove from heat. Carefully discard the thyme stems, allspice berries and scotch bonnet pepper.
In a large sauce pan over medium heat, add oil and heat until ready. Then add onions, garlic, scallions, thyme, scotch bonnet pepper, sugar (if using), black pepper, and allspice berries. Saute.

Once onions are soft (about 2-3 minutes), turn heat up to medium-high. Add in the kidney beans, rice, coconut milk and water then stir together. Bring to a boil, cover, then continue to simmer until rice is fluffy and liquid is soaked up(about 25-30 minutes). Remove from heat. Carefully discard the thyme stems, allspice berries and scotch bonnet pepper.

allergenSubs.to: oat cream + 1 tsp olive oil (per 1 cup)

title: Jamaican Chickpea Curry
rawTitle: ### Jamaican Chickpea Curry — Sweet Potato Soul
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://sweetpotatosoul.com/jamaican-curry-chickpeas/ ✓ VERIFIED
- $1.40/serving · **Menu price:** $11–14 · 320 g · 380 cal · 14 g · 45 min · 4 · **Easy** · 🥘 ⚡
description: Curried chickpeas in a rich coconut-tomato gravy with thyme and scotch bonnet. Plant-based by default; pairs with rice and peas or roti.
ingredients: 2 tablespoons avocado oil (or. extra virgin olive oil or coconut oil)
2 tablespoons Jamaican curry powder (or regular curry powder)
1 ½ teaspoons sea salt or kosher salt
3 cups cooked chickpeas (or two 14-oz cans, drained and rinsed)
4 small gold potatoes (about ¾ lb) (cubed (peeling optional))
2 teaspoons vegetable Better Than Bouillon paste (or, swap 1 1/2 cups vegetable broth for the water and bouillon paste)
1 green onion (green parts only, sliced for garnish)
steps: Warm the oil in a large, deep skillet over medium heat. Add the curry powder and toast (bloom) for about 20 seconds.
Add the onion, garlic, Scotch bonnet pepper (if using), ginger, and ½ teaspoon of the salt. Stir well and sauté for 3–5 minutes, until the onion is tender.
Add the chickpeas, potatoes, carrots, coconut milk, water, bouillon paste, thyme, and the remaining 1 teaspoon salt. Stir to combine.
Bring the curry to a simmer, then cover and cook for about 20 minutes, or until the potatoes and carrots are tender.
Season to taste with additional salt and pepper.
Serve over brown rice or with vegan naan. Garnish with sliced green onion and cilantro.

allergenSubs.to: oat cream + 1 tsp olive oil (per 1 cup)

title: Cuban Black Beans
rawTitle: ### Cuban Black Beans — Loving It Vegan
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://lovingitvegan.com/cuban-black-beans/ ✓ VERIFIED
- $0.85/serving · **Menu price:** $4–6 (side) / $10–12 (entrée bowl) · 240 g · 280 cal · 12 g · 60 min · 6 · **Easy** · 🥘 ⚡
description: Black beans slow-stewed with sofrito of onion, pepper, and garlic, finished with cumin, oregano, and a splash of vinegar. Naturally vegan; gets better day two.
ingredients: 3 tbsp coconut oil ((44ml) can use olive oil)
1 tbsp raw cane sugar ((15g) optional, can use coconut sugar )
additional pink salt and black pepper to taste, if needed
steps: Start by rinsing the beans several times before leaving them to soak overnight in a large pot or bowl of water (not the 6 cups of water).
The next day drain off the dark coloured water and replenish with the 6 cups of water (add the beans to a large pot if you have used a bowl during the soaking process).
Add the bay leaves and a tablespoon of coconut oil to the pot.
Bring the water to the boil then reduce the heat to medium and simmer with the lid on until they soften (this may take up to 1-1 ½ hours).
Once the beans have cooked, they should be soft to the touch (use the back of a spoon, to determine tenderness) if ready, it will mash with ease.
Prepare the sofrito by sautéing the garlic, onions and bell peppers in a skillet with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil on medium heat until soft and translucent.
Season the sofrito mix with the black pepper, oregano, cumin and pink salt then stir.
Once the onions have turned translucent, transfer the contents into the large saucepan with the black beans and stir.
Use a ladle to extract some of the beans (about ¾ cups worth) and mash them before returning them to the pot. This will help to create a thick bean sauce.
Stir in the cider vinegar (and sugar if you are using) before reducing to low-medium heat.

Put the lid on the pot and allow to simmer until the beans start to thicken up (anything from 45 minutes- 1hour). Feel free to mash some more beans during the time of simmering for further thickening or add a splash of water if required.
Do a taste test and adjust for additional seasoning if required
Discard the bay leaves and serve accordingly

allergenSubs.to: refined avocado oil or vegan butter

title: Caribbean Pelau
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://healthiersteps.com/vegan-pelau/ ✓ VERIFIED
- $1.95/serving · **Menu price:** $12–15 · 380 g · 460 cal · 14 g · 60 min · 6 · **Easy** · 🥘 ⚡
description: One-pot rice with pigeon peas, coconut milk, and burnt-sugar caramelization for the signature deep amber color. Trinidad's national one-pot — built for batch service.
ingredients: 1 can dried. pigeon peas (2 cups dried pigeon peas, rinsed and drained)
½ cup green seasoning (blend of fresh herbs like thyme, cilantro, scallions, garlic)
steps: Caramelize the Sugar: In a heavy-bottomed 5-quart pot, heat the brown sugar over high heat until it froths, bubbles, and darkens. This step creates the characteristic smoky sweetness of pelau.

Cook the Mushrooms, Peas, and Rice: Add the chopped king oyster mushrooms, pigeon peas, and rinsed rice to the pot. Stir well, allowing the mixture to cook for 3 minutes, absorbing the caramelized sugar flavor.
Add Vegetables and Seasonings: Mix in the cubed carrot, red bell pepper, pumpkin, celery, onion, and green seasoning. Season with salt and black pepper.

Simmer with Coconut Milk: Pour in the coconut milk and water, ensuring the mixture is covered by at least an inch of liquid. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and cover the pot. Let it simmer for 30 minutes or until the rice is fully cooked and the liquid has evaporated.
Final Taste and Serve: Once cooked, fluff the rice with a fork and adjust seasoning if necessary. Serve hot with fried plantains, coleslaw, or a green salad for a complete meal.

allergenSubs.to: oat cream + 1 tsp olive oil (per 1 cup)

title: Jamaican Patties (Cruelty-free)
rawTitle: ### Vegan Jamaican Patties — Make It Dairy Free
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://makeitdairyfree.com/vegan-jamaican-patties/ ✓ VERIFIED
- $1.95/serving · **Menu price:** $4–6 ea / $10–13 (2-pc plate) · 180 g · 380 cal · 10 g · 90 min · 8 · **Medium** (assembly labor; par-bake) · 🥘
- **Premium row:** Beyond Beef in patty filling +$1.10
ingredients: 1 cup unsalted vegan butter, (cut into 1 tbsp sizes)
1 pound vegan beef ((frozen crumbles or vegan ground beef))
1 scotch bonnet pepper, (carefully remove seeds and ribs, very finely diced (sub habanero if needed))
2 teaspoon garam masala ((or sub curry seasoning))
steps: In a large bowl, whisk together 3 1/2 cups flour, 1 tbsp each: sugar, turmeric, and Jamaican curry powder, and 1 tsp salt. Then, add 1 cup unsalted vegan butter in tablespoon size cubes. Combine by using a fork or pastry cutter until crumbly, sand like texture forms.

Add in 3/4 cup of water and mix with a spatula to form a dough. A 1 tbsp more at a time with remaining water if needed to form dough, do not over add water. Only mix until combined. Place in fridge to set up for 30 minutes.
In a large skillet over medium low heat, add 1 tbsp oil and saute 1 large diced onion until translucent (about 3-5 mins). Add 2 cloves minced garlic and cook 1 more minute until fragrant.

Add 3 stalks cut green onions and 1 lb frozen meatless crumbles. Stir together and cook for 2-3 minutes. Do not overcook. Add in 1 scotch bonnet pepper, tsp garam masala, 1 tsp each brown sugar, dried thyme, and all purpose seasoning, 3/4 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp each ground ginger, black pepper, and allspice, and 1/4 tsp onion powder. Stir well.

Add 1 cup stock and let cook for 15-20 minutes on simmer (medium low heat) until liquid has reduced and mostly gone. Add in 1/4 cup vegan butter in and stir until melted. Pour in 1/4 cup panko and stir. If still juicy, add 1 tbsp more at a time. *May not need all of the Panko. Don’t want it to be dry. Let meat mixture cool to warm. Do not add hot mixture to pastry dough.
Preheat oven to 400˚F. Get a small bowl of water. Set aside.

Evenly split dough into 12 equal pieces. Lightly flour surface. Roll a piece into a ball, then roll into a circle with a rolling pin to 6 inches in diameter. Lightly brush the edges with water (very light).*We prefer to use a scale for accuracy.
Add 3-4 tbsp of meat mixture to bottom half of the dough circle. Fold until edges line up and use a fork to seal the edges. Place on baking tray. Repeat with remaining dough and meat mixture.
Bake for 25-28 minutes on until golden and crispy. Remove and let cool for at least 5 minutes. Serve warm.

title: Jerk Chunk Steak Platter with Rum Glaze (Cruelty-free)
rawTitle: ### Vegan Jerk Chunk Steak Platter with Rum Glaze (**SHOWSTOPPER**)
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://makeitdairyfree.com/authentic-jamaican-jerk-tofu-recipe/ ✓ VERIFIED *(Andrew Bernard / Make It Dairy Free — fully authentic Jamaican jerk paste from scratch (pimenta berries, scotch bonnets, ground thyme, scallions, ginger, lime, soy, molasses, garam masala). **Recommended swap: Chunk Steakhouse Cut 6 oz** in place of the tofu; finish with reduced dark-rum-tamarind-brown-sugar glaze at the pass. The original recipe's tip on reducing leftover marinade with cornstarch into a glaze applies directly.)*
- **Description:** **Lead protein:** Chunk Foods Steakhouse Cut 6 oz, marinated 24 hr in scratch jerk paste (scotch-bonnet, allspice/pimento, thyme, scallion, ginger), pimento-wood smoked or grilled, then glazed at the pass with reduced dark-rum-tamarind-brown-sugar lacquer. Plated with rice & peas, fried sweet plantains, charred pineapple salsa, and a shot of pickled scotch-bonnet escovitch. The Caribbean steakhouse plate.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $8.20 / serving · **Menu price:** **$26–32** (upscale Caribbean ÷ 0.27–0.30)
- **Weight:** 460 g · **Calories:** 720 · **Protein:** 36 g · **Time:** 24 hr marinade + 25 min cook · **Servings:** 1
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Hard** — multi-component plate; per-ticket grill + glaze. Plantains and rice & peas hold; salsa and escovitch cold-prep daily.
- **Tags:** 🥘 (sides + marinade) · — (per-ticket grill)

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| **Lead protein:** Chunk Steakhouse Cut 6 oz (default) | Best jerk-grill caramelization | baseline |
| Beyond Steak Tips on skewers | Casual-tier jerk-espetinho | −$2.40 → menu $19–22 |
| Jerk-marinated tofu (existing dish) | Vegan-traditional | −$5.90 → existing $13–16 listing |

Plated with rice & peas, fried sweet plantains, charred pineapple salsa, and a shot of pickled scotch-bonnet escovitch. The Caribbean steakhouse plate.

title: Caribbean Sweet Potato Pudding (Cruelty-free)
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://healthiersteps.com/vegan-jamaican-sweet-potato-pudding/ ✓ VERIFIED *(Michelle Blackwood, RN / Healthier Steps — Jamaica-born Caribbean food blogger, fully vegan + gluten-free, 4.99/61 votes (exceptionally well-tested). Recipe is naturally vegan from the ground up — uses canned coconut milk in place of dairy and an extra coconut-milk custard topping added at the halfway-bake mark for the signature golden custard top. The Jamaican baking style is called "hell a top, hell a bottom, and hallelujah in the middle" — both rack positions on hot, custard set in the middle. Notable: best made with the white-flesh batata variety (West Indian sweet potato), but readers report success with American sweet potato if you double the flour. AP flour also works as a sub for the gluten-free flour per reader testing.)*

  **Recipe** (12 slices, per Michelle Blackwood): 2 lb sweet potatoes (preferably batata; if using American sweet potato, double the flour), 1 × 14 oz can coconut milk, 1 cup coconut palm sugar, 1 tsp freshly grated ginger, 1 tsp vanilla, 1 cup gluten-free AP flour or brown rice flour (or regular AP), ½ tsp cinnamon (optional), ½ tsp nutmeg (optional), ¼ tsp sea salt. **Custard topping:** ½ cup coconut milk + 1 tbsp coconut sugar + ¼ tsp vanilla + pinch cinnamon. Preheat 375°F, grease 9" round pan. Peel + chop sweet potatoes; grate or process in a food processor (add a little of the coconut milk to help it process). Transfer to a large bowl; add coconut milk, sugar, ginger, vanilla, spices, salt; stir in flour to form a mostly-smooth batter. Pour into pan; bake 45 min. Whisk custard topping ingredients; pour evenly over the half-baked pudding; return to oven another 45 min until top is golden brown. Total: 1 hr 45 min. 166 cal/slice.
- **Description:** Slow-baked grated-sweet-potato pudding with coconut milk, ginger, and warming spices, finished with a coconut-milk custard topping that bakes into a golden custard glaze. Sliced like a cake, plated warm with a coconut-rum cream drizzle or vegan coconut ice cream. The classic "hell a top, hell a bottom, and hallelujah in the middle" Jamaican pudding (the traditional name for the dual-rack baking method), naturally vegan.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $1.20 / serving · **Menu price:** $7–10 / $9–12 with rum cream
- **Weight:** 130 g (slice) · **Calories:** 166 · **Protein:** 2 g · **Time:** 15 min prep + 1 hr 30 min bake · **Servings:** 12 / 9" pan
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Easy** — bake whole, slice, warm in salamander per ticket. Holds 1–2 days well-wrapped at room temp; freezes excellently.
- **Tags:** 🥘 Bulk-Prep · ⚡ Fast-Service

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| Batata (white-flesh Jamaican sweet potato — Michelle's default) | Most authentic — sold at West Indian, Asian, African groceries; also Walmart | baseline ($1.20) |
| American sweet potato (orange-flesh) — double the flour | More widely available; brighter color | flat |
| Purple sweet potato | Striking color, premium plating | +$0.40 |
| Add coconut-rum cream drizzle (per-ticket) | Premium presentation | +$0.30 → menu $9–12 |
| Pair with vegan coconut ice cream | À la mode | +$0.80 → menu $9–12 |
description: Slow-baked grated-sweet-potato pudding with coconut milk, ginger, and warming spices, finished with a coconut-milk custard topping that bakes into a golden custard glaze. Sliced like a cake, plated warm with a coconut-rum cream drizzle or vegan coconut ice cream. The classic "hell a top, hell a bottom, and hallelujah in the middle" Jamaican pudding (the traditional name for the dual-rack baking method), naturally vegan.
difficultyNote: bake whole, slice, warm in salamander per ticket. Holds 1–2 days well-wrapped at room temp; freezes excellently.

subs.from: Batata (white-flesh Jamaican sweet potato — Michelle's default)
subs.effect: Most authentic — sold at West Indian, Asian, African groceries; also Walmart

subs.from: American sweet potato (orange-flesh) — double the flour
Pair with vegan coconut ice cream

ingredients: 1 cup gluten-free all purpose flour (or brown rice flour)
steps: Preheat oven 375℉. Grease a 9-inch round baking pan. Set aside.
Peel sweet potatoes and roughly chop. Grate using the grater or a food processor in batches. If using a food processor or blender, add some coconut milk to help process.
Transfer grated sweet potatoes to a large bowl, add coconut milk, coconut palm sugar, spices and salt.
Stir in flour to form a mostly smooth batter.
Pour batter into cake pan and bake for 45 minutes.
Combine all topping ingredients in a small bowl and pour evenly on the top of the pudding.
Return pudding to oven and bake for another 45 minutes or until top is golden brown.
Delicious served alone or with coconut whipped cream.
Please double the flour if using the regular American sweet potato variety.

allergenSubs.to: oat cream + 1 tsp olive oil (per 1 cup)
